operating_state: Total revenue increased 8% to $13.3 billion driven by 7% capacity expansion and sustained travel demand strength across all geographic regions, particularly for premium products. Operating income improved $891 million year-over-year to $614 million as revenue growth significantly outpaced cost increases. Corporate travel sales to managed customers grew 14% led by technology, consumer services and financial services sectors, while American Express cash sales increased 5% to $1.7 billion.
cost_structure: Total operating costs per available seat mile decreased 6% primarily due to capacity leverage and absence of prior year one-time pilot agreement expenses. Non-fuel unit costs increased 1.5% driven by higher employee wages and profit sharing accruals. Operating expense growth of 1% was substantially below revenue growth, reflecting operational efficiency gains from scale expansion.
material_events: Delta entered definitive agreement to purchase 20 Airbus A350-1000 aircraft with option for 20 additional widebody aircraft, with deliveries beginning 2026 to enhance international fleet fuel efficiency and premium capacity. The aircraft order falls within previously announced capital expenditure and capacity targets.
macro_exposures: No material changes from previously disclosed risk factors were reported during the quarter. Standard exposures to fuel costs, weather disruptions, geopolitical events, and regulatory changes remain unchanged from prior disclosures.
forward_outlook: Management reaffirmed full-year 2024 guidance of $6-7 earnings per share and $3-4 billion free cash flow with adjusted debt-to-EBITDAR ratio of 2x-3x. March quarter revenue growth expected in top half of 3-6% guidance range with approximately 5% operating margin and $0.25-0.50 earnings per share.
uncertainty_profile: Forward-looking projections remain subject to standard airline industry volatilities including fuel cost fluctuations, weather disruptions, and competitive dynamics. Mark-to-market adjustments on equity investments create earnings variability based on market conditions and foreign currency movements.
